mapping.a_element: financial time series analyzed (S&P 500 sliding-window returns fed into a Takens embedding)
mapping.b_element: financial variable modeled (log stock price dynamics)

mapping.a_element: Rayleigh-quotient minimization of the combinatorial-Laplacian loss (a variational optimization)
mapping.b_element: maximization of entropy subject to information constraints (a variational inference principle)

brief_justification: The two only share the financial application domain and a loose 'variational principle' label; their actual machinery is unrelated. Paper A estimates topological invariants (Betti numbers) by variationally minimizing a Rayleigh quotient over a combinatorial-Laplacian null space on a quantum register, whereas Paper B uses maximum-entropy/entropic inference to derive a Merton jump-diffusion stochastic process (Kolmogorov-Feller/Fokker-Planck, Esscher transform) for option pricing, so no role-for-role correspondence of the driving mechanism holds.
